"you can help me by correcting every mistake I had made" - The sentence is grammatically correct, but you might want to say "you can help me by correcting every mistake I make" to make it more clear. This is because the word "had" implies that the mistakes happened in the past, while "make" implies that the mistakes will happen in the future.
